channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: ChatGPT and Gemini are fighting to be the AI bot that sells you stuff
item.description: The AI-powered shopping rivalry is heating up as Google and OpenAI launch new features to help you buy things while interacting with their chatbots. Now, Google is teaming up with Gap Inc to allow...

item.title: # Sorted Squares of a Sorted Array (Java) – Efficient Two-Pointer Approach
item.description: When working with sorted arrays, a seemingly simple transformation—like squaring each element—can break the order. This problem is a great example of how understanding patterns in data can help us...

item.title: ⛓️‍💥 AI Agents broke my Dev setup
item.description: I had a setup that worked perfectly for me, 3 screens (Actually 2 screen + Macbook Pro) one with IDE (WebStorm, Vscode and from time to time just sublime). Second with Terminal (started with...

item.title: Postgres pipelines from the JVM with Bpdbi
item.description: Every time your JVM app talks to Postgres, something wasteful happens. Your code sends a query, waits for the response, sends the next query, waits again. Each wait is a full network round-trip...

item.title: Deploying AG-UI Agents to Production with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
item.description: The Agent-User Interaction Protocol (AG-UI) standardizes how AI agents communicate with user applications. But deploying those agents means the same manual work: configuring SSE endpoints, writing...

item.title: Arm Is Now Making Its Own Chips
item.description: The chip design firm says Meta, OpenAI, Cerebras, and Cloudflare are among the first customers of its new artificial intelligence hardware.

item.title: Welp, I bought an iPhone again
item.description: It takes a week to switch phones. First, there's the technical process of moving eSIMs across devices, which takes either a few minutes (if you're switching from one Android phone to another) or...

item.title: Stop Guessing Why Your App is Slow: A Pragmatic Guide to Big O Notation
item.description: Let’s be real for a second. When we start coding, our main goal is just making things work. If the feature ships and the bugs are squashed, we’re happy. But then, your user base grows, your...

item.title: 25 Dicas de performance com .NET 10
item.description: O .NET 10 traz melhorias significativas de performance, mas conhecer as técnicas certas faz toda a diferença. Este artigo reúne 25 dicas práticas para extrair o máximo de suas aplicações. 1....

item.title: # Finding First and Last Occurrence in a Sorted Array (Java)
item.description: When working with sorted arrays, one common problem is to find the first and last occurrence of a given element. This becomes especially interesting when the array contains duplicate values. In...

item.title: How to Run NemoClaw with a Local LLM & Connect to Telegram (Without Losing Your Mind)
item.description: I just spent a full day wrestling with NemoClaw so you don’t have to. NemoClaw is an incredible agentic framework, but because it is still in beta, it has its fair share of quirks, undocumented...

item.title: AI's Infrastructure & Agents: From Chips to Code Automation
item.description: AI's Infrastructure & Agents: From Chips to Code Automation Today's Highlights This week, we dive into critical advancements shaping AI development, from groundbreaking solutions for inference...

item.title: I'm Building: AgentGuard360: Free Open Source AI Agent Security Python App
item.description: I've been posting on Reddit about an open source agent security tool I'm building called AgentGuard360, and I thought I'd share information about it here as well. What makes this app unique is its...

item.title: This scientist rewarmed and studied pieces of his friend’s cryopreserved brain
item.description: L. Stephen Coles’s brain sits cushioned in a vat at a storage facility in Arizona. It has been held there at a temperature of around −146 degrees °C for over a decade, largely undisturbed. That...

item.title: 5 Learnings from the First-Ever Gartner Market Guide for Guardian Agents
item.description: On February 25, 2026, Gartner published its inaugural Market Guide for Guardian Agents, marking an important milestone for this emerging category. For those unfamiliar with the various Gartner...

item.title: Hackers Use Fake Resumes to Steal Enterprise Credentials and Deploy Crypto Miner
item.description: An ongoing phishing campaign is targeting French-speaking corporate environments with fake resumes that lead to the deployment of cryptocurrency miners and information stealers. "The campaign uses...

item.title: A BlackBerry you might actually want just landed on Kickstarter
item.description: When The Verge's Allison Johnson went hands-on with the company's Titan 2 Elite phone at MWC 2026, Unihertz didn't divulge pricing info. But the compact Android phone with a BlackBerry-inspired...

item.title: Meet the former Apple designer building a new AI interface at Hark
item.description: The company said it would design models, hardware, and interfaces in tandem to deliver a "seamless end-to-end personal intelligence product."

item.title: Life is Strange: Reunion is a full-circle moment for its stars
item.description: Fans thought they were done with the original Life is Strange duo of Max and Chloe. Following the first game and its 2017 prequel, players said their tearful goodbyes to these beloved characters -...

item.title: Google’s Android Automotive is moving from the dashboard to the ‘brain’ of the car
item.description: Google announced a new version of its Android Automotive open-source operating system for software-defined vehicles. Whereas previously Android Automotive operated exclusively in the car's...

item.title: Apple Maps will introduce ads this summer
item.description: Apple will soon allow businesses to buy advertisements in its Maps app. In an announcement on Tuesday, Apple says ads may appear at the top of your search results in Maps, as well as in a new...

item.title: Pokémon Champions is coming to the Nintendo Switch on April 8th
item.description: Right now, Pokopia has everyone busy building houses and beautifying the world, but next month, we'll be making our pocket monster friends fight for sport in Pokémon Champions. Today, Nintendo and...

item.title: Meta’s reckoning over kids safety is in the hands of two juries
item.description: Two juries are currently deliberating a series of cases that could either usher in a legal reckoning for Meta, or maintain the status quo in an uphill battle to impose changes or penalties on tech...

item.title: Fortnite maker Epic is laying off over 1,000 workers
item.description: Layoffs have hit Fortnite developer Epic Games again. Today CEO Tim Sweeney announced that the company is cutting more than 1,000 jobs, citing a downturn in engagement for Fortnite dating back to...

item.title: John Deere, Garmin, and Philips may have undermined military right to repair
item.description: Last year, Congress dropped widely supported military right-to-repair provisions from the annual defense policy bill - and now we may know who was pushing them to do it. Recently released lobbying...

item.title: Mirage raises $75M to continue building models for its AI video-editing app Captions
item.description: Mirage, the maker of video-editing app Captions, has raised $75 million in growth financing from General Catalyst's Customer Value Fund (CVF).

item.title: The Download: tracing AI-fueled delusions, and OpenAI admits Microsoft risks
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The hardest question to answer about AI-fueled delusions...

item.title: Agile Robots becomes the latest robotics company to partner with Google DeepMind
item.description: Agile Robots will incorporate Google DeepMind's robotics foundation models into its bots while collecting data for the AI research lab.

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Cybersecurity Specialization: Losing Foundational Skills
item.description: Cybersecurity has changed fast. Roles are more specialized, and tooling is more advanced. On paper, this should make organizations more secure. But in practice, many teams struggle with the same...

item.title: Ghost Campaign Uses 7 npm Packages to Steal Crypto Wallets and Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a new set of malicious npm packages that are designed to steal cryptocurrency wallets and sensitive data. The activity is being tracked by ReversingLabs as...
